ending: Silvertide has destroyed Wizen's local puppets and badly damaged the Evergreen essence in his possession, but Wizen remains active and his objective involving the Torrins is unknown. Jalen agrees only to investigate, while Father remains a self-interested source and Karina's obligations are unresolved. Noah is a rank-four mage with a perfect Natural Disaster rune, a suppressive domain, and a violin that can channel two rune effects at once. Moxie continues rebuilding and teaching, and the students have entered the advanced track with their individual pattern work underway. Raphael is dead after Azel took the Inquisitor's bloodline curse and died from it. Lee has advanced to rank four, but Azel's restraint on her dangerous demon rune is only temporary. Emily accepts Lee without hesitation, Alexandra remains cautious but does not reject her, and Brayden orders the group to hide the death. Renewal and Decras continue watching them, with Decras delaying another planned threat until the conflict with Wizen is resolved.
in_short: Noah returns to Arbitage, perfects his runes with Sunder, and reaches rank four with Natural Disaster while Jalen's assassination campaign shifts into uneasy interest. Noah and Moxie bring their students into the advanced track and teach them to develop formations from deeply understood patterns. Meanwhile, Lee rebuilds her runes but fears the change required for a demon's advancement. Wizen infiltrates Arbitage through plant-controlled corpses, killing Will and using Mayreena as another puppet while holding Evergreen's essence. Silvertide destroys Wizen's local network and damages Evergreen, but Wizen survives. Inquisitor Raphael then exposes Lee and nearly kills Moxie. Lee advances to rank four to save her, and Azel manifests, kills Raphael, takes the fatal bloodline curse, and dies after temporarily restraining Lee's unstable rune. The group conceals Raphael's death as Wizen and a postponed divine test remain ahead.

recaps.text: Previously: Noah Vines has been living publicly as Vermil Linwick, the magus he killed after arriving in this world. At Arbitage, he teaches Todd, Isabel, and Emily while researching runes with Sunder, which allows him to divide combined runes and rebuild them. Moxie, his partner and fellow professor, was bound to the Torrin family until the death of Evergreen released her. Lee, their demon companion, has become part of the same close group. Their allies include the powerful Silvertide, while Azel remains tied to Lee and Noah's group. Karina, a Linwick shapeshifter once known as Bria, has a complicated history with Noah, and the Linwick and Torrin families remain dangerous powers around the group. Todd is recovering from a severe neck injury, Isabel hides a valuable inherited rune, and Emily does not yet know the full truth about her family or Noah's identity.

Noah, Moxie, and Lee finish their vacation by hunting monsters and gathering runes. Moxie claims Earthen Muster, a perfect Earth Master Rune, while Noah collects the ingredients needed to rebuild his rank-three foundation. Back at Arbitage, three visitors bring new obligations. Alexandra asks to become Noah's student after escaping Gentil's control. Contessa, formerly Evergreen's aide, needs shelter and becomes Noah's paid informant. Karina carries a summons from Jalen, the rank-six Linwick family head, who knows artifacts vanished from the family catacombs. Noah refuses to enter Linwick territory and demands neutral ground instead. Jalen responds by sending an assassin, but Karina kills the attacker before he reaches Noah. Noah meanwhile completes four focused rank-three runes - Sandstorm, Tsunami, Cyclone, and Earthquake. He returns to teaching with greater strength, but Jalen's demands have become an active campaign against him.

Noah reunites with Todd, Isabel, and Emily, admits Alexandra on probation, and temporarily includes James, a student of Revin. On Windscorn Plateau, each student defeats a monster alone. Alexandra reveals that permanent rank-three body runes give her exceptional durability and sword skill but prevent her from advancing further. Noah then offers the Earth Master Rune as the prize in a longer training competition. The first challenge is simply to touch Lee while she evades. The students divide into two groups and use hunted monsters as food bait, but Lee defeats both traps and nobody scores. Noah explains that all five could have cooperated and chosen together who most needed the prize. Back at Arbitage, he separates Todd, Isabel, and Emily from the others and secures their promises of secrecy before beginning private mind-meld sessions. Their lessons are about to move from ordinary training into Noah's most closely guarded rune knowledge.

In private mind-melds, Isabel reveals Soul, the inherited Master Rune that once drew deadly noble attention. Noah discloses Sunder's ability to separate combined runes and helps her create a perfect stone rune. Todd learns the same secret and replaces an overcomplicated concept with a perfect Steam rune. Emily's session forces broader truths into the open: Evergreen was her mother, Moxie had been held under Torrin control, and Noah killed both Evergreen and the original Vermil. Emily accepts Noah's truth-bound account and begins perfecting her own runes. Jalen's assassins continue to die, leading him to abandon disposable attackers in favor of a direct meeting. Seeking components for Noah and Lee, the group gains access to a masked noble auction. With about forty thousand gold and Sunder's ability to salvage flawed runes, Noah plans to turn unwanted purchases into useful materials. The auction opens under strict anonymity, giving the group room to bluff about its wealth and influence.

At the auction, Noah cultivates the appearance of wealth, buys powerful but flawed runes for later dismantling, and attracts Number 14, an anonymous bidder seeking rare monster runes. Number 14 trades a shadow-and-light rune lot suited to Lee, sixty-five thousand gold, and a future contact patch for Chitinus Spine Armor. Noah also acquires fire and ice runes needed for his own advancement. Garina and Ferdinand attend while searching for an unknown powerful target, and a later shared meal exposes their conflicting beliefs about power, fear, and care without producing a fight. Noah then uses Sunder to build flawless Volcano, Blizzard, and Thunderstorm runes. He combines seven perfect rank-three disasters into a rank-four Natural Disaster rune. At the same moment, Jalen arrives personally over the stolen Records of the Deceased. Noah's new domain resists the rank-six pressure better than anyone expected, but Jalen overwhelms the room and strikes him after Mascot's intervention turns the confrontation volatile.

Jalen transports Noah to the Linwick estate, but the threatened punishment becomes an unstable personal interest. He accepts that the undefended catacombs were a game Noah won, leaves the Records of the Deceased with him, and spends two hours playing darts before returning him unharmed. Noah confirms that Natural Disaster is genuinely perfect and discovers that his new domain senses nearby life while suppressing magic. He begins teaching the students that formations can grow from any thoroughly understood pattern, with Todd choosing fire, Isabel and Alexandra swordwork, Emily ice, and James music. Lee learns from Azel that demon advancement changes the demon rune itself and may alter her personality, but resolves to prepare for rank four so she can protect the group. Elsewhere, Ferdinand abandons his Church search rather than leave Garina. Renewal loses control of the red-horned cat avatar, while Decras joins her in watching the unknown mortal responsible. As Noah proves his rune's strength, Arbitage invites his group into its competitive advanced curriculum.

Noah discovers seven concealed rune positions in his violin and learns to cast two loaded effects at once through music. Silvertide confirms that the advanced track is real, prestigious, and likely to draw noble resentment toward Noah's students. Lee then asks Noah to rebuild her runes. They perfect her ordinary set, heal the resulting damage, and add a broken dark Master Rune without an immediate adverse effect. The advanced track tests Noah through Ulya's remote swordsman and accepts him after he destroys it. Todd, Isabel, Emily, Alexandra, and James also choose to join and begin demonstrating personal combat patterns. Gero privately tests Alexandra, whose nonmagical sword pattern cuts through his defenses. Will tests Moxie more aggressively and is forced to concede after she overwhelms him. In response, Noah creates a masked benefactor persona, defeats Will without killing him, and confronts Ulya. She disavows Will's coercion and agrees that future evaluations should follow ordinary limits, leaving Noah with both leverage and a possible channel into the advanced track.

Ulya discovers that Will is already dead and that a plant construct has been wearing his body. The construct attacks her and tries to prepare her as another replacement, but she escapes and brings Gero into the investigation. Wizen is revealed as the controller of several such bodies and the captor of Evergreen, whose runes he plans to consume. Ulya turns to Noah because his masked persona claimed knowledge of similar puppet magic. Noah connects the attack to Wizen's earlier use of Evergreen and joins the investigation, while the advanced track formally accepts his group. Seeking better intelligence, he visits Father at the Linwick estate. Father identifies Wizen as a powerful mind mage obsessed with the Torrins and explains that he can gradually prepare control over a person's body. By examining a puppet, Noah realizes replacement constructs should lack normal heartbeats and blood flow. Father promises to obstruct Wizen and send Brayden, but remains a self-interested ally. A second danger arrives as Raphael follows a blood trail into Arbitage while hunting a demon.

Noah quietly screens his students for heartbeats while continuing pattern lessons. Jalen follows the class to Windscorn Plateau, recognizes that their movements resemble formations, and agrees to investigate Wizen while keeping Noah's teaching from the noble families. At the advanced-track welcome gathering, Noah detects that leader Godric has no heartbeat and suspects another replacement. Using his masked Spider persona, he warns Ulya that one clone is present. Silvertide arrives after finding another plant-piloted corpse and publicly conceals what he knows. He later explains that Godric survives through an artificial heart and identifies Mayreena as the true puppet. Silvertide recruits Noah and Ulya to follow her. When cornered, Mayreena admits responsibility and deploys more wooden bodies. Silvertide then reveals his own silver-thread puppet, extends his domain through the surrounding network, and destroys Mayreena along with roughly twenty hidden puppets. The sympathetic strike reaches Evergreen's captive essence and shatters several of her runes. Wizen survives, but his local network is gone and he must change tactics.

With Wizen likely to retaliate, Noah and Moxie continue strengthening the students and preparing them for the advanced track's demanding standards. Raphael watches the group, suspects that one of Noah's associates is the demon he seeks, and forces Tim to send him to Windscorn Plateau. There he approaches Moxie, Lee, Emily, and Alexandra under the pretense of testing for possession. His rosary exposes Lee, while his rank-five blood domain suppresses Moxie and leaves her helpless. Lee turns back rather than escape and, at Azel's insistence, advances to rank four. Her new demon form breaks the immediate restraint and lets her stop Raphael's first attack, but he fires a bloodline curse at Moxie to exploit Lee's protective instinct. Lee takes the curse instead. Azel then manifests, kills Raphael, and draws the curse into himself. He admits that Lee's protective interpretation of Gluttony ruined his plan to claim her rune. Before dying from the curse, he restrains her unstable new rune and calls her his daughter.

After Raphael's death, Lee tells Moxie that Azel died protecting her. Emily accepts Lee immediately after learning she is a demon, while Alexandra remains wary but does not abandon her. Moxie buries Raphael and keeps his rosary. Brayden arrives in response to Tim's alarm, provides healing potions, judges that the Inquisitor probably acted alone, and instructs the group to conceal the death. Lee is now rank four, but fears that her new demon rune may eventually turn her against the people she advanced to protect. Azel's final intervention has only delayed that danger. Renewal and Decras observe the aftermath and believe Noah may be able to repair the flawed rune. They also confirm that Wizen remains a separate and immediate threat. His local puppet network has been destroyed and Evergreen's essence damaged, but he is still active. Decras postpones another planned test until Noah's group has dealt with Wizen, leaving the survivors stronger but facing both an unfinished mortal conflict and later divine interference.
